summary: Brent crude swung violently this week — from under $100 to $119 and back toward $112 as continued attacks on Persian Gulf energy infrastructure compounded fears of a U.S. ground invasion, with the Pentagon deploying three warships and over 2,000 more Marines to the region. The Fed held rates at 3.50–3.75% but raised its 2026 inflation forecast to 2.
tweet: Iran war keeps oil near $112/bbl; the Fed hiked its inflation forecast and markets now price zero cuts in 2026 — bonds and stocks feel it across the board.
